_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in providing geolocation information utilizing target infrastructures.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in building and maintaining data service catalogs that document available data products, access methods, service level expectations, ownership, and support channels for data consumers.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI OP domain. OP-S-009 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DG-S-008 (DG, cosine: 0.391), TF-S-011 (TF, cosine: 0.385).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.1/10 (raw 0.412). Shared vocabulary: [skill]. DCWF-only terms [geolocation, information, infrastructures, providing, target, utilizing] vs WIDAI-only terms [access, available, building, catalogs, channels, consumers] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.849 (P=0.852, R=0.846). Balanced P/R — the 8-word DCWF element and 25-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (95.8%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Skill in providing geolocation information utilizing target infrastructures." and "Skill in building and maintaining data service catalogs that document available". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.397 ≈ STS 0.412 (gap 0.015). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [skill] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 7.4%. Shared tokens: [skill]. DCWF-only: [geolocation, information, infrastructures, providing, target, utilizing]. WIDAI-only: [access, available, building, catalogs, channels, consumers].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 95.8% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.
